revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:چیکو آمی]][[ja:竹阿弥]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1550|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|spouse={{ill|Ōmandokoro|qid=Q5353737|1=Ōmandokoro}}|children={{ill|Asahihime|qid=Q838180|1=Asahi no kata}}; {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|qid=Q1191183|1=Toyotomi Hidenaga}}}}
{{Infobox samurai | name = Takeami | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Chikuami, Yasuke | titles = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = | shogunate = | lord = {{ill|Oda Nobuhide|en|Oda Nobuhide|qid=Q1153962|lt=Oda Nobuhide|1=Oda Nobuhide}} | domain = | clan = {{ill|Mizuno clan (Kammu Heishi)|ja|水野氏 (桓武平氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno clan (Kammu Heishi)|1=Mizuno clan (Kammu Heishi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11549416}}?<ref>『[https://lab.ndl.go.jp/dl/book/10301741?keyword=%E7%AD%91%E9%98%BF%E5%BC%A5&page=7諸系譜 第2冊]』による。</ref> | parents = Father: {{ill|Mizuno Tameharu|ja|水野為春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tameharu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}? | siblings = | spouse = {{ill|Ōmandokoro|en|Ōmandokoro|qid=Q5353737|lt=Ōmandokoro|1=Ōmandokoro}} (Naka) | children = {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|Toyotomi Hidenaga|qid=Q1191183|lt=Toyotomi Hidenaga|1=Toyotomi Hidenaga}}, {{ill|Asahi no kata|en|Asahi no kata|qid=Q838180|lt=Asahi-hime|1=Asahi no kata}} | notable_facts = }} '''Takeami''' (竹阿弥, Chikuami) was a figure of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}. According to traditional accounts, he was the second husband of Naka ({{ill|Ōmandokoro|en|Ōmandokoro|qid=Q5353737|lt=Ōmandokoro|1=Ōmandokoro}}), the mother of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} (Hōkō, Tōkichirō). This made him Hideyoshi's stepfather and the biological father of {{ill|Toyotomi Hidenaga|en|Toyotomi Hidenaga|qid=Q1191183|lt=Toyotomi Hidenaga|1=Toyotomi Hidenaga}} (Koichirō) and {{ill|Asahi no kata|en|Asahi no kata|qid=Q838180|lt=Asahi-hime|1=Asahi no kata}} (Suruga-gozen). However, as detailed below, there are contradictions within this account. His name is also written as '''Chikuami'''. == Biography == The origins of Takeami are debated.<ref>{{ill|Takasawa Hitoshi|ja|高澤等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasawa Hitoshi|1=Hitoshi Takasawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11671897}}, referencing the 'Taikō Soseiki' in his article for {{ill|Rekishi Yomihon|ja|歴史読本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekishi Yomihon|1=Rekishi Dokuhon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11546891}}, speculated that the Hideyoshi family may have identified with the Mizuno clan due to their use of the omodaka crest, Hideyoshi's name 'Tōkichirō', and familial connections, suggesting that Takeami, believed to be a stepfather, may have belonged to the Mizuno lineage. However, the validity of this claim is uncertain.</ref> Few concrete details about him remain. The {{ill|Taikō Soseiki|ja|太閤素生記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō Soseiki|1=Taikō Sujō-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21015430}} describes Takeami as a retainer of {{ill|Oda Nobuhide|en|Oda Nobuhide|qid=Q1153962|lt=Oda Nobuhide|1=Oda Nobuhide}}, born in {{ill|Nakamura (Nagoya)|ja|中村 (愛知県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura (Nagoya)|1=Naka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11365156}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}.<ref>Now part of {{ill|Nakamura-ku, Nagoya|en|Nakamura-ku, Nagoya|qid=Q780521|lt=Nakamura Ward|1=Nakamura-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|qid=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}.</ref> It states that he retired due to illness, returned to his hometown, and married Naka, the widow of Hideyoshi’s father, becoming an adopted husband (nyūboku). {{Quotation|One of Oda Nobuhide’s dōhōshū (monastic attendants) was Takeami, born in Nakamura. Due to illness, he withdrew to Nakamura, where he married the widow of Kinoshita Yaemon, Hideyoshi’s mother, and had a son and daughter.|『太閤素生記』{{sfn|Owada|1985|p=36|qq=}}|qq=}} The 'Taikō Soseiki' presents Takeami as the stepfather of Hideyoshi and his sister, {{ill|Tomo (Toyotomi)|en|Tomo (Toyotomi)|qid=Q5353767|lt=Nichihide|1=Tomo (Toyotomi)}}, and the biological father of Hidenaga and Asahi-hime. However, this claim has inconsistencies. Hidenaga was born in 1540 ({{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21096812}} 9), and Asahi-hime in 1543 (Tenbun 12), the same year as the death of Yaemon, Hideyoshi’s purported father. If so, Takeami could not have been their biological father, and it is more plausible that all four children were Yaemon's offspring.<ref>{{sfn|Owada|1985|pp=66-67|qq=}}</ref> Other sources such as the 'Bishū Shiryaku' assert that Takeami was a stepfather, but that Hideyoshi was an illegitimate child of a monk from Rengeji Temple in Hachisuka-go.<ref>{{sfn|Watanabe|1919|p=11|qq=}}</ref> Meanwhile, the 'Heihō Shōsetsu Ben' by {{ill|Takizawa Bakin|en|Takizawa Bakin|qid=Q463142|lt=Kyokutei Bakin|1=Takizawa Bakin}} claims Hideyoshi had no legitimate father and refers to Takeami as a 'temporary father' (kafu).<ref>{{Citation |author=Taki Sawakai|year=1889|chapter=Heihō Shōsetsu Ben|title=Kyokutei Zatsuki|publisher=Atsumi Masamochi|url={{NDLDC|992968/28|qq=}} |qq=}}</ref> == Related Works == ; Television Drama * {{ill|Hideyoshi (given name)|en|Hideyoshi (given name)|qid=Q5752746|lt=Hideyoshi (NHK Taiga Drama)|1=Hideyoshi (given name)}} (1996, NHK Taiga Drama), portrayed by {{ill|Zaitsu Ichirō|ja|財津一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaitsu Ichirō|1=Ichirō Zaitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634619}}. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Citation |last=Taniguchi|first=Katsuhiro|author-link=Taniguchi Katsuhiro|year=2011|title=Nobunaga, Hideyoshi, and Their Vassals|publisher=Gakken Publishing|ISBN=9784054050211|qq=}} * {{Citation|last=Owada|first=Tetsuo|author-link=Owada Tetsuo|year=1985|title=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|series=Chūō Kōron Shinsho|isbn=4121007840|qq=}} * {{Citation |last=Kose|first=Boan|author-link=Kose Boan|year=1919|title=History Compilation Collection|publisher=Kondō Publishing|url={{NDLDC|3431173/233|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Citation |last=Watanabe|first=Seiyū|author-link=Watanabe Seiyū|year=1919|title=Toyotomi Hideyoshi and His Family|publisher=Japan Academic Association|url={{NDLDC|953289/22|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|竹阿弥|version=104506390|comment=Imported full ja history}}
